title: Rest In Peace Tay Keith
intro: Tay Keith was a Memphis producer who passed away on June 18, 2026. He was BlocBoy JB’s go-to producer, and in 2018 they secured a Drake feature on *Look Alive*, which changed their lives forever. If I remember correctly, Drake announced he was dropping a music video with BlocBoy JB at a time when no one knew who he was, we just wanted new drake. It was an instant hit and EVERYONE started doing that dance. You really couldn’t go a day without seeing someone do it. In addition to the dance, Tay Keith’s tag and riser sfx instantly become recognizable as his signature sounds. His tag quickly reached the same tier of recognition of other hot producers like:  Ronny J, CashMoneyAp, Mike WiLL Made-It, Murda Beatz, TakeADayTrip.

Even though he was the hottest producer in 2018, he faced heavy criticism in the community for his beats being ‘too simple’ and ‘too repetitive’.  I can understand where they’re coming from, but Tay Keith’s ear proved to be his true talent as he was able to evolve his sound and continue to make hits year after year. After blowing up with *Look Alive*, Drake continued to make countless hits with Tay Keith. Some songs include: *Sicko Mode*, *Never Recover*, *Nonstop*, and *Over The Top*. He went to work with many other artists and his popularity appeared to be dropping. But his career would see another peak by producing breakout hits from Sexy Redd. To name a few, he did: *Pound Town*, *Get it Sexy*, *SkeeYee*, and *Bow Bow Bow*. On top of all that, he stayed loyal and produced a couple of the diss tracks for Drake and at least one song on all the new Drake albums. Outside of Drake and Sexy Redd, *EVERY CHANCE I GET* and *STOOPID* were big moments produced by Tay Keith.

If I were to give him a nickname, it would be: Tay “Strike-While-The-Iron’s-Hot” Keith. Rest in peace  to one of my favorite producers. In the rest of this post I’ll attach some of my favorites that don’t get enough love.

songs.title: Temptation
songs.review: Not only is this my most played song of 2019, this is my most played song since I've had Apple Music. This is largely due to Tay Keith's production.

songs.title: Promise U That
songs.review: I imagine this song being temptation's twin brother. This beat is very similar, but the actual lyrics and chorus are stronger here. Every part of this song is very screamable

songs.title: Really Like That
songs.review: This is different from a typical Tay Keith beat and I love it.

lines.text: In here fuckin' with Tay Keith, but like Tay-K, I did the race (Yuh)

lyrics.caption: I love when rappers shout out the producer in songs

lines.text: Shout out my lil' mans, call him Xan 'cause he slump niggas (Slump niggas)
I might pull up Ghost, Rolls, I been bought the truck, nigga
Know you know that sayin', if it's up there then it's stuck, nigga (Stuck)
Hit 'em in his head, he ain't get up but he stuck, nigga (He stuck)

lyrics.caption: There was a moment in time when you couldn't avoid some variation of: up and it's stuck

songs.title: Same Thing
songs.review: One of the best songs on Lil Baby's best album? Yes. This beat independently has all the elements you would expect from Tay Keith. But at a faster tempo than usual, it feels like a fresh sound from him.

songs.title: Don’t Come Out the House
songs.review: Another big moment in his breakout year. Him and Metro work very well together on this
songs.searchQuery: not all heroes wear capes metro

songs.title: Mother’s Daughter
songs.review: There was a brief and I mean brief time where Miley Cirus perfectly balanced her hip hop experiment and making good music. It was here on this ep. It might get lost in her discography, but I'd recommend listening to this 6 song ep if you haven't already. She knew to start it off with the strongest song here. Also, no surprise that this is one of the few songs you wont hear Tay Keith's iconic tag.
